contracts: Summit Trail Software License Renewal Agreement FY2026

Twelve-month renewal of the 120-seat AsterPrint management suite license for Summit Trail Software, requested ahead of the current term expiration. The renewal quote was raised under support case CAS-260134 and customer success lead Jae Park has confirmed budget approval pending final signature. Pricing holds the prior-term rate with a 3 percent uplift and adds the remote configuration service pack.
Granite Peak Manufacturing Maintenance Services SOW

Statement of work covering quarterly preventive maintenance for production-floor print and labeling equipment at the Erie plant, including the label printers adjacent to line three. Coverage includes a four-hour on-site response commitment, which was exercised during the line three spindle vibration downtime tracked as case CAS-260132. Plant maintenance lead Tom Okafor is the named site contact.
Riverbend Medical Group Master Services Agreement

Master services agreement governing managed print, document capture, and on-site service for all Riverbend Medical Group clinic locations. Includes patient-privacy handling terms for device hard-drive sanitization at end of lease and names Priya Natarajan as the customer relationship owner. Order-level pricing is set by attached schedules rather than this master document.
Bluegrass Credit Union Master Services Agreement

Master services agreement for branch printing and secure document workflows across all Bluegrass Credit Union branches. Contains audit-support and chain-of-custody terms for member document handling, with Marcus Webb signing as member services manager. Renewal is automatic annually unless notice is given ninety days before term end.
Prairie Wind Energy Sensor Deployment SOW

Draft statement of work for deploying office environment sensor kits across Prairie Wind Energy Cooperative control rooms and district offices. Scope was expanded after the substation feeder fault telemetry review under case CAS-260128 to include export integration testing with grid operations supervisor Elena Vasquez. Awaiting customer legal review of the data-sharing appendix.
City of Alder Creek Managed Print Services Agreement

Municipal managed print services agreement covering city hall, the permit office, and the public records annex. The current term expires this quarter and city clerk Dana Whitfield has initiated the procurement cycle for renewal, with pricing held flat contingent on council approval. Includes public-records compliant retention of print job logs.
Harbor Lights Grocery Refrigeration Monitoring SOW

Statement of work adding sensor kit monitoring and alert label printing for refrigeration cases at the Tacoma store. The cold chain temperature excursion in the produce section, case CAS-260133, triggered the expanded alerting scope now covered here. Store operations manager Rosa Delgado receives the weekly exception report.

Campus-wide master services agreement for managed print and course-material production across Lakeview University libraries, departments, and the student print center. Includes semester-based volume tiers and an accessibility clause for alternate-format course packets. HR benefits administrator Harper Lindqvist coordinated the employee print benefit rider.
Beacon Hill Staffing Partners Services Renewal

Draft renewal of document services for Beacon Hill Staffing Partners, expanded to cover contractor onboarding paperwork kits. Finalization is blocked on the contractor onboarding background check issue tracked as case CAS-260135, since the onboarding packet volume drives the pricing tier. Talent operations director Nina Kowalski is reviewing the draft.
Federal Plaza Services Agency Task Order Agreement

Task order agreement for secure document production and grant reporting support services at Federal Plaza Services Agency. The current review cycle incorporates corrective language after a grant drawdown report was rejected for a missing form, tracked as case CAS-260136. Contracting officer representative Samuel Greer holds signature authority.
Willow Brook Legal Records Management SOW

Statement of work for matter-file records management services at Willow Brook Legal, including shelf delivery, labeling, and quarterly archive box rotation. Records manager Talia Stone signs off on each rotation manifest. Confidentiality terms incorporate the firm's client-matter privilege requirements by reference.
Silver Elm Logistics Fleet Print SOW

Statement of work covering route manifest and freight label printing infrastructure across Silver Elm Logistics distribution hubs. Includes replacement hardware provisioning for the Denver hub and a document capture pilot for proof-of-delivery scanning. Route manager Miles Vega coordinates hub scheduling windows.
Harbor Pine Consulting Master Services Agreement

Expired master services agreement for workbook production and engagement document services at Harbor Pine Consulting. The term lapsed last month and engagement manager Felix Xu has asked for a refreshed draft rather than an extension, citing changed engagement volumes. Historical pricing schedules are retained for reference only.

Renewal of course packet production and training room equipment services for Foxglove Learning. Program director Hugo Bell requested the index-page quality checks added as a standard step after last term's course packet issues. Renewal pricing reflects a reduced signage scope following the training room signage cancellation.
policies: Employee Benefits and Open Enrollment Policy 2026

Defines employee benefits elections and the 2026 open enrollment window running January 5 through January 30. Employees must confirm elections in the benefits portal before the window closes; the help desk maintains a known-issues list for portal login failures during peak enrollment, the same class of issue seen in customer support case CAS-260137. Late elections default to prior-year coverage.
Paid Time Off and Leave Policy

Sets accrual rates, carryover limits, and request procedures for paid time off, sick leave, and parental leave. Accruals post on the first business day of each month and unused carryover above five days lapses at fiscal year end. Managers approve requests in the HR portal within three business days.

Covers reimbursable business expenses, receipt thresholds, and submission deadlines. Expense reports are due within thirty days of the expense date, itemized receipts are required above twenty-five dollars, and mileage is reimbursed at the published standard rate. Finance rejects reports that mix personal and business charges on a single line.

Governs issuance and use of corporate purchasing cards, including single-transaction limits and prohibited merchant categories. Currently under review to raise the field technician transaction limit for on-site parts purchases and to add a monthly reconciliation checklist. Card misuse triggers immediate suspension pending finance review.
Information Security and Acceptable Use Policy

Establishes acceptable use of company systems, password and multifactor requirements, and incident reporting duties. All staff must report suspected phishing within one hour of discovery and complete annual security awareness training. Device encryption is mandatory for laptops and any device storing customer document previews.

Defines how customer document content, contact records, and service history may be stored, shared, and retained. Customer files stay within approved document libraries, exports require manager approval, and synthetic or anonymized data must be used in all demos and testing. Violations are treated as security incidents.

Master retention schedule for contracts, invoices, service records, and personnel files. Executed contracts are retained for seven years after expiration, service case records for five years after closure, and meeting notes for two years unless attached to an active contract. Legal hold overrides all scheduled disposition.

Sets eligibility, equipment, and availability expectations for remote and hybrid work arrangements. Under review to clarify core collaboration hours and home-office equipment reimbursement caps ahead of the next fiscal year. Field service roles remain on-site by default with dispatch-based scheduling.
meetingnotes: Renewal sync with Summit Trail Software covering the FY2026 license renewal quote requested under case CAS-260134. Jae Park confirmed budget approval and asked for the remote configuration service pack to be itemized separately on the final quote. Action: send the revised renewal agreement for signature before the expiration date.

Quarterly service review at the Erie plant. Walked through the line three unplanned downtime postmortem for case CAS-260132 and confirmed the spindle-adjacent label printers are now on the quarterly preventive maintenance rotation under the active SOW. Tom Okafor asked for vibration readings to be attached to future visit reports.
Harbor Lights Cold Chain Escalation Standup

Escalation standup for the produce section cold chain temperature excursion, case CAS-260133, with the related aisle four refrigeration alarm CAS-260138 reviewed in the same session. Sensor alert thresholds were retuned and Rosa Delgado will receive the weekly exception report going forward. Follow-up visit scheduled for the Tacoma store.

Pipeline review of the Riverbend Medical Group managed print fleet refresh opportunity. Deal was closed lost this cycle; clinic leadership deferred capital spend while the prior authorization SLA issue in case CAS-260124 remains open, and Priya Natarajan asked to revisit next quarter. Action: keep the fleet assessment current and re-engage after case closure.

Working session with grid operations on the substation feeder fault flagged in the telemetry export, case CAS-260128. Elena Vasquez walked through the export format and the team mapped which sensor kit fields feed the district dashboards. The draft sensor deployment SOW will add an export integration test before signature.

Debrief on document support for the disputed card transaction investigation, case CAS-260126. Marcus Webb confirmed the chain-of-custody terms in the master services agreement cover the statement reprints requested by the member services team. Action: deliver certified copies within the audit-support turnaround in the MSA.

Monthly check-in with the city permit office. The building permit application backlog in case CAS-260130 is driving higher plan-set printing volumes than the current managed print agreement forecast, which strengthens the renewal case Dana Whitfield is taking to council. Action: prepare a volume-tier comparison for the renewal packet.

Weekly pipeline review across open opportunities. Cedar Hollow Printing managed print fleet refresh and Blue Heron Stationery preventive maintenance program both advanced to the propose stage, while the Willow Brook Legal office sensor deployment is holding at develop pending a floor survey. Action: schedule the Willow Brook survey and refresh close dates on all three.

Preparation session for the resubmission of the grant drawdown report rejected for a missing form, case CAS-260136. Samuel Greer confirmed the corrected form list and the task order agreement language now names the reporting support deliverables explicitly. Action: route the updated task order draft to agency review this week.

Support huddle on the open enrollment benefits portal login failures at Lakeview University, case CAS-260137. Harper Lindqvist reported the failure spike aligns with the enrollment window close and asked for extended support coverage through the deadline. Action: staff extra support hours and share the known-issues workaround list.
